personal.name: Ahmed Maher
personal.tagline: Repurposing Legacy Hardware & Exploring System Security
personal.background: Linux Enthusiast | Hardware Revivalist | Security Researcher
personal.bio:
I’m the kind of person who can’t see a piece of old tech without wondering how to bring it back to life. My journey started with 'modding' a school tablet and fixing family phones, and it led me to building full-scale home servers from what others called 'junk' hardware.

Currently a 3rd-year Computer Science student at HTI, I refuse to be boxed into a single 'track.' I dive into Backend, explore Cyber Security, and mess around with Video Editing. To me, it’s not about the title; it’s about solving problems and understanding how technology works under the hood. If a system needs a workaround or an idea needs to be executed smartly and on a budget—I’m the one for the job.
personal.description: Professional portfolio of Ahmed Maher, a passionate Software Developer specializing in Full-Stack Development & Web Solutions.
personal.keywords: portfolio, web developer, projects, Ahmed Maher, Software Developer, Full-Stack Development

system-architecture.title: System Architecture
system-architecture.description: Reviving old hardware, building home servers, and Linux system administration.

projects.title: Project Phoenix: Transforming Old Android into a Linux Home Server
projects.subtitle: An engineering experiment to upcycle e-waste into a fully functional 24/7 Home Lab & NAS
projects.description: Instead of throwing away my old Android tablet, I decided to challenge myself: Can this become a real server? I rooted the device, replaced the Android restrictions with a full Linux environment (Ubuntu), and built a complete Home Lab.

cyber-security.title: Cyber Security
cyber-security.description: Documenting TryHackMe challenges, Kali Linux experiments, and system vulnerability research.

software-dev.title: Software Development
software-dev.description: Building projects with C++ and Python, focusing on clean code and core fundamentals.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio Website
projects.subtitle: Responsive portfolio showcasing skills and projects
projects.description: A modern, fully responsive portfolio website built from scratch using HTML5, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript.

mobile-modding.title: Mobile Modding
mobile-modding.description: Hardware repairs, custom ROMs, and advanced Android automation via Termux.

innovation.title: Business & Innovation
innovation.description: Exploring entrepreneurship and digital transformation through programs like innovEgypt (ITIDA).

the-lab.title: The Lab
the-lab.description: A collection of experimental scripts and works-in-progress from my daily tinkering.

timeline.title: More Than Just Games
timeline.description: Got my first PC. While everyone was gaming, I was busy asking: "How does this work? What happens if I change this setting?

timeline.title: The Digital Window
timeline.description: Middle school started, and we finally got internet at home. The PC became more than a machine; it was my window to an endless world of learning.

timeline.title: Breaking the System
timeline.description: In High School, the school tablet was my first real challenge. "How do I get this out of the restricted system?" That was my first true taste of hacking and system logic.

timeline.title: Linux in My Pocket
timeline.description: Discovered Termux and managed to run Kali Linux on my phone. It changed my perspective of mobile devices from simple gadgets to powerful programming tools.

timeline.title: Solid Foundations
timeline.description: Decided to go academic. I started with C++ to understand the low-level fundamentals that separate a 'coder' from a real Engineer.

timeline.title: The HTI Exploration
timeline.description: Joined HTI and started a deep dive into everything—Backend, Security, and Entrepreneurship. Certifications from TryHackMe and innovEgypt (ITIDA) are just markers on a path of constant exploration.
